channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya Disburses Over Sh2 Billion to Landowners for Electricity Transmission Projects
item.description: The Kenyan government has disbursed Sh2.23 billion as compensation to landowners affected by major electricity transmission projects, though Sh182 million is still pending. Energy CS Opiyo Wandayi rep...

item.title: JOOTRH Records Fewer Deaths After Expanding Specialist Services and Modern Equipment
item.description: At Jaramogi Oginga Odinga Teaching and Referral Hospital (JOOTRH) in Kisumu, patient deaths have gone down significantly following strategic investments in specialist care, modern equipment, and staff...

item.title: HRT patch shortages in Australia: practical alternatives and safety advice
item.description: Australia has been facing an ongoing shortage of oestrogen hormone replacement therapy (HRT) patches since 2020, with supply issues expected to continue at least until the end of 2026. These patches a...

item.title: Ted Turner, CNN Founder and Media Pioneer, Dies Aged 87
item.description: Ted Turner, the influential media entrepreneur who transformed television and founded CNN, has passed away at the age of 87. Turner is renowned for creating the first 24-hour news network in 1980, a r...

item.title: Princess of Wales launches new early childhood development resource at University of East London
item.description: The Princess of Wales visited the University of East London to launch a pivotal stage of her early years campaign. During her visit, she observed research into the social and emotional development of ...

item.title: Quebec’s liquor board criticized for aggressive sales strategies amid declining alcohol consumption
item.description: In Quebec, the state-run liquor retailer, the Société des alcools du Québec (SAQ), is facing mounting scrutiny from public health experts and advocacy groups. Despite a notable 3.6 per cent decline in...

item.title: Lara Intervenes During Allan and Marcus's Heated Argument in Blood vs Duty Episode 23
item.description: In Episode 23 of the popular Filipino drama series 'Blood vs Duty,' tensions rise as Allan and Marcus get into a heated argument, putting their professional and personal relationships to the test. Lar...

item.title: PDP Chieftain Explains How Money Influences Politics in Nigeria
item.description: Chief Nname Robinson Ewor, one of the top people for the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) for Rivers State, don yan say Naija politics still dey driven by money, no matter how some people wan talk other...

item.title: Historical Protestant Support for Birth Control Focused on Family, Not Feminism
item.description: In the mid-20th century, many Protestant leaders in the United States supported birth control not as a tool for women's liberation but as a means to promote what they called 'responsible parenthood.' ...

item.title: Saskatchewan NDP MLA Betty Nippi-Albright Leaves Party to Sit as Independent
item.description: Betty Nippi-Albright, a Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) for the Saskatchewan NDP, has announced she will now sit as an Independent. In a social media statement, Nippi-Albright cited her inabi...

item.title: Central Government Plans to Encourage Bengal and Tamil Nadu to Sign PM-SHRI MoU Soon
item.description: The Central Government is reportedly planning to formally request the governments of West Bengal and Tamil Nadu to sign the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) under the PM-SHRI initiative. The PM-SHRI ...

item.title: Andrew Muhlach confirms authorities have identified car break-in suspects and filed charges
item.description: Actor Andrew Muhlach recently confirmed that the authorities have successfully identified the suspects involved in the break-in of his car. Following the incident, a formal case has already been filed...

item.title: Thunder Dominate Lakers in Game 1 with Strong Defense and Holmgren’s Breakout Performance
item.description: In Game 1 of the Western Conference semifinals, the Oklahoma City Thunder overwhelmed the Los Angeles Lakers 108–90, primarily due to their stifling defense. While the Thunder shot efficiently—49% fro...

item.title: CNN Founder Ted Turner Dies at 87 After Transforming Global Media
item.description: Pumanaw na si Ted Turner, ang founder ng CNN at isang kilalang media mogul, sa edad na 87 ayon sa ulat ng CNN. Wala pang ibinigay na opisyal na sanhi ng kanyang pagkamatay, ngunit noong 2018 ay inihay...

item.title: CDF Asim Munir Honoured for Efforts Towards Peace and Stability at Paigham-i-Islam Conference
item.description: ISLAMABAD – At the Sixth International Paigham-i-Islam Conference held recently, Chief of Defence Forces and Army Chief Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir was awarded the titles of 'Leader of Peace' and 'G...

item.title: Olisa Agbakoba Advocates Section 162 Reform To Stop Revenue Losses in Nigeria
item.description: Senior Advocate of Nigeria, Olisa Agbakoba, don raise alarm say Nigeria dey suffer serious federation account wahala wey dey cause the country lose around ₦20 trillion every year. For one press briefi...

item.title: University Graduate Receives Support from Well-Wishers After Emotional Graduation with Mother
item.description: Nokuthula Dlamini, a 24-year-old student, recently graduated from the University of KwaZulu-Natal with a Bachelor of Arts Honours degree in Sociology. Her graduation moment, where she broke down in te...

item.title: Ramaphosa Visits Zimbabwe to Discuss Constitutional Amendment Concerns and Deputy Firing
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa recently visited Zimbabwe, reportedly to caution President Emmerson Mnangagwa against pursuing the controversial Constitutional Amendment Bill No.3, which could...

item.title: Bam Aquino Supports BINI as Philippine Tourism Ambassadors
item.description: Senator Bam Aquino has publicly expressed his support for the proposal to appoint the popular girl group BINI as tourism ambassadors of the Philippines. Aquino highlighted that promoting tourism throu...

item.title: The Life and Drawings of Nobuo Kiuchi, Japanese POW in Ukraine
item.description: After the end of World War II, Japanese soldier Nobuo Kiuchi spent two and a half years in Soviet prisoner-of-war camps in Ukraine. Captured after Japan's surrender, he was sent to work in Donbas, in ...
